current_status: Full project is still in documentation/framework design; deep meta progression is specified through client/data/meta_progression.json; strong SaveManager requirements now cover meta and run saves; docs/TODO.md tracks the human-readable future task backlog; MVP client has reached M4.6 with centralized mvp_config.json tunables; AI knowledge base v2 and three-layer AI memory are active; AI large-change auto-commit policy and context-compaction task recovery rule are established; eight local OpenCode skills and .agents/skills/game-ai-reference are active; GodotPrompter, headless-godot-skill-kit, and Claude-Code-Game-Studios are retained only as vendor reference sources under .opencode/vendor/ai-resources; external plugins, hooks, active .claude tools, templates, starter projects, examples, and production state are excluded.
last_completed: AI knowledge base v2 with docs_health_check
Experience and level-up choice system decision (ADR #43)
AI memory split into long-term index, current_state.json, and session logs (ADR #44)
MVP M4.6 centralized player, input, weapon, enemy, spawner, and background tunables in MinimumViableProduct/client/data/mvp_config.json
Full project numeric and localization configuration manuals established in client/data/README.md and client/locale/README.md (ADR #45)
Full project deep meta progression specified with meta currency, account level, permanent upgrades, unlocks, and SaveManager meta persistence boundary (ADR #46)
Human-readable future task backlog established in docs/TODO.md
Minimal Stage 1 CI enabled in .github/workflows/docs-check.yml for JSON, CSV, docs health, and whitespace checks (ADR #47)

Strong player save requirement established: SaveManager must support meta progression saves and run suspend/resume saves with migration, atomic writes, backup fallback, and broken-save quarantine (ADR #48)

Seven creative/strategy subagents added across CodeBuddy, Codex, and OpenCode: game-designer, numeric-designer, ip-designer, copywriter-packager, ui-art-designer, game-art-designer, marketing-strategist (ADR #49)

Communication rule added: when asked for issues/risks, answer factually and do not invent problems; when new requirements arrive, first assess project fit, cost-effectiveness, complexity, and risks (ADR #50)

Data validation, contract sync, and lightweight Godot Bridge landed: tools/sync_contracts.py, tools/validate_data.py, tools/godot_bridge.py, generated _contracts.json and client/scripts/contracts/*.gd, and docs-check CI now runs sync + validate + docs health (ADR #51)
AI Git commit policy established: large changes are auto-committed after status/diff/log checks and verification; minor changes are not auto-committed; only task-owned files may be staged (ADR #52)

Eight project-level OpenCode skills installed under .opencode/skills for Godot/GDScript, scene validation, Godot test diagnostics, doc sync, safe git commit, factual code review, AI resource curation, and MCP tool evaluation (ADR #53)

Context-compaction task recovery rule established: after summaries or context restoration, user last explicit instruction overrides summaries, Next Steps, and candidate current_state actions (ADR #54)

GodotPrompter, headless-godot-skill-kit, and Claude-Code-Game-Studios installed as review-only Git submodules under .opencode/vendor/ai-resources; they are not in OpenCode skills.paths and are not auto-enabled (ADR #55)

External AI tools formally installed while excluding templates/examples: GodotPrompter enabled via local OpenCode plugin, headless-godot installed under .agents/skills and added to OpenCode skills.paths, and Claude-Code-Game-Studios agents/skills/hooks/rules installed under .claude (ADR #56; active layer later superseded by ADR #58)

CCGS cross-agent adapter installed at .agents/skills/ccgs-game-studio so non-Claude agents can reuse .claude/agents and .claude/skills on demand without bulk-registering all CCGS agents (ADR #57; active layer later superseded by ADR #58)

External AI library active layer simplified to .agents/skills/game-ai-reference: GodotPrompter plugin, .agents/skills/headless-godot, .agents/skills/ccgs-game-studio, and active .claude tools are removed; vendor submodules remain as reference sources (ADR #58)
next_actions: MVP M5 playtest and workflow/feel/config-tuning review
Use docs/TODO.md as the human-readable backlog when choosing future tasks
Use advisory subagents for gameplay design, numeric modeling, IP, copy, UI art, game art, and marketing strategy discussions

Use OpenCode project skills for Godot/GDScript work, scene validation, Godot test diagnostics, doc sync, safe commits, factual review, AI resource curation, and MCP evaluation; use .agents/skills/game-ai-reference when external GodotPrompter, headless-godot, or CCGS references are needed; restart OpenCode after skill/config changes
Decide whether MVP needs an M6 iteration after M5 review
Start full client M1 autoload skeleton when ready, including strong SaveManager for meta and run saves before MetaProgressionSystem implementation
CI stage 1 follow-up: commitlint, local pre-commit hook, and incremental watch loop
notes: Long-term decisions remain authoritative in docs/决策记录.md.
Use python tools/sync_contracts.py after changing docs/词表与契约.md, then run --check.
Use python tools/validate_data.py after changing client/data, client/locale, or MVP config.
Use python tools/godot_bridge.py for MVP scene-tree export, data validation, Godot version checks, and headless boot.
Do not over-optimize or invent findings when the user asks for issues; say no issue when no actual issue is found.
When the user proposes a requirement, briefly assess project fit, value, cost-effectiveness, complexity, and major risks before implementing.
Detailed daily history remains in docs/AI记忆/会话日志/.
Do not read or modify draft/ or DRAFT/ unless explicitly authorized.
For large AI changes, create a git commit after checking status, diff, recent log, and verification; for minor changes, explain why no commit was made.

Project-level OpenCode skills live in .opencode/skills; .agents/skills/game-ai-reference is the only active cross-tool adapter for external AI library references; source/evaluation notes live in docs/AI协作/AI技能资源评估.md; restart OpenCode after changing skills paths or config.

External AI resource packages live in .opencode/vendor/ai-resources as upstream submodules; plugins, hooks, active .claude tools, templates, starter projects, examples, and production state are intentionally not installed.

After context summary, compaction, or restoration, align to the user's last explicit instruction first; summaries, Next Steps, and current_state next_actions are candidate context only, not authorization.
